JavaScript Core (70) - ES6 Template Literals: Writing HTML with Emmet Inside Template Literals in VSCode
This one's a quick note about working with Template Literals over in VSCode.
Writing HTML with Emmet inside template literals
When we write HTML, most of us lean on Emmet, so you've probably got a bunch of shortcuts in muscle memory. For example, typing
and hitting Tab spits out this:

The thing is, in VSCode that Emmet expansion doesn't fire inside a template literal by default. To make it work in there, you need to tweak a setting. I'm not going to walk through it again here — I already covered it in an earlier post, so just check that out:
Handy VSCode settings worth using (article in Mandarin)
Once that's set up, you can type the same thing inside a template literal, hit Tab, and watch it expand:
Emmet expanding inside a template literal in VSCode
